latest.title: Share Incentive Plan Purchase
latest.content:
Today 17:56
RNS Number : 7285G
Ondo InsurTech PLC
02 June 2026
2 June 2026
Ondo InsurTech Plc
("Ondo" or the "Company")
Share Incentive Plan Purchase
Director/PDMR Shareholding
Ondo InsurTech Plc (LSE: ONDO), a leading provider of claims prevention technology for home insurers, received notification on 1 June 2026 from Fiduchi Trustees Limited (UK) (the "SIP Trustee") in relation to the purchase of 114,848 Ondo ordinary shares of 5 pence each ("Ordinary Shares") for the benefit of participants of the Ondo Share Incentive Plan (the "SIP"), which includes Craig Foster and Kevin Withington, both Directors of the Company, outlined below in accordance with the rules of the SIP (the "SIP Award").
The SIP is an all-employee trust arrangement approved by HM Revenue and Customs, under which each participating employee will acquire Ordinary Shares at the prevailing market price per share using contributions deducted from pre-tax salary in each calendar month ("Partnership Shares"), with each participating employee being awarded one Ordinary Share for each Partnership Share ("Matching Shares") also acquired by the SIP.
Details of the number of Partnership Shares purchased by each of the Directors at a price of 3.50 pence per Ordinary Share, and the number of Matching Shares allocated by the SIP Trustee to the participants for no consideration, is set out against their names in the table below.

latest.title: Vendor Loan Note Restructuring
latest.content:
28 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 9557F
Ondo InsurTech PLC
28 May 2026
The information contained in this announcement is deemed by the Company to constitute Inside Information as stipulated under the Market Abuse Regulations (Regulation 596/2014/EU) as it forms part of UK Domestic Law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018
28 May 2026
Ondo InsurTech Plc
("Ondo", the "Company", or the "Group")
Vendor Loan Note Restructuring
Ondo InsurTech Plc (LSE: ONDO), a leading provider of claims prevention technology for home insurers, is pleased to announce the rescheduling of vendor loan notes issued by Ondo ("Loan Notes") to its largest shareholder, HomeServe Assistance Limited ("HomeServe").
The Loan Notes form part of the consideration for the acquisition of the LeakBot business from HomeServe as set out in the prospectus dated 21 March 2022.
The Loan Notes remain redeemable by Ondo at any time with 10 days' notice and are rescheduled as follows:
·
Deferral of principal repayment of £1.6m due 31 March 2027 to 30 September 2028.
Given this adjustment, it was agreed that the interest rate would increase to 15% from 1 June 2026 and then to 17% from 31 March 2027.
As previously announced, the Group requires short term funding and the Board is actively exploring a range of financing options, including by way of an equity fundraising. Further announcements will be made in due course.
Mark Wood CBE, Executive Chairman of Ondo InsurTech Plc, says
: "HomeServe recognises the value of the LeakBot franchise in the USA, Scandinavia and the UK. The rescheduling to September 2028 meaningfully strengthens our financial position, as we work towards a comprehensive solution to our short-term financing needs, ensuring we can continue to support rapid growth in each region. We appreciate HomeServe's ongoing confidence in our future."
Enquiries
For further information, please visit
www.ondoplc.com
or contact the following:
Ondo InsurTech Plc
Craig Foster, CEO
Kevin Withington, CFO
+44 (0) 800 783 9866

latest.title: Appointment of Non-Executive Director
latest.content:
21 May 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 1694F
Ondo InsurTech PLC
21 May 2026
21 May 2026
Ondo InsurTech Plc
("Ondo" or the "Company")
Appointment of Non-Executive Director
Ondo InsurTech Plc
(LSE: ONDO), a leading provider of claims prevention technology for home insurers (the "Company"), is pleased to announce the appointment of Graham Bird as Non-Executive Director of the Company with immediate effect.
Graham brings over 30 years of experience spanning financial advisory, investment management and executive leadership. A chartered accountant who qualified with Deloitte in London, Graham has worked extensively in advisory, investment, commercial and financial roles. Graham has built a plural career in the public markets, having served as Chief Financial Officer of XP Factory plc and previously held Non-Executive Director roles at, Journey Group plc, Rockwood Strategic plc, Fireangel Safety Technology plc, SpaceandPeople plc and Universe Group plc.
Graham brings a strong background in financial reporting, performance analysis, business strategy, and corporate finance, including M&A, debt and equity funding. He brings experience in investor relations and has a proven commitment to enabling sustainable business growth and driving value for stakeholders in dynamic and growing sectors. Prior to XP Factory, Graham was one of the founding employees at fund management boutique Gresham House plc where, in addition to supporting the company's growth, he was responsible for establishing and managing the successful strategic equity business unit, which focused on both quoted and unquoted equity investments.
Before this, Graham spent six years in senior executive roles at PayPoint Plc. He was also head of strategic equity investment at SVG Investment Managers, having previously been at JPMorgan Cazenove, where he served as a director in the corporate finance department.
In addition to his Non-Executive Director duties, Graham has also entered a consulting agreement with the Company whereby he will work closely with the Finance team to provide additional support to the Company's finance function during this period of financial repositioning.
Mark Wood, Executive Chairman of Ondo InsurTech Plc, commented:
"
We are delighted to have attracted a Non-Executive Director of Graham's experience. His appointment is a strong endorsement of Ondo's technology and model. As we work to scale our proposition Graham's insights and expertise both as a Board member and as a consultant to the Company will be invaluable."
Graham Bird commented:
"
I am excited to be joining the team at Ondo InsurTech at this important moment in the Company's evolution. Protected by its patented technology and a compelling use case, Ondo has made significant progress in developing a leading role in a market with enormous opportunity. As with many small businesses, there are challenges scaling and financing growth, which I am confident Ondo has the capability to overcome providing the opportunity for significant future commercial success."
With effect from his appointment, Graham Bird will serve as a member of the Audit Committee.
This announcement is made in accordance with the UK Listing Rules and the Company confirms that there is no information to be disclosed under Listing Rule 6.4.8R other than as set out in this announcement.
Enquiries
For further information, please visit www.ondoplc.com or contact the following:
Ondo InsurTech Plc
Craig Foster, CEO
Kevin Withington, CFO
+44 (0) 800 783 9866
Singer Capital Markets

Anastassiya Eley

latest.title: Change of Board Responsibilities
latest.content:
23 Apr 2026 11:01
RNS Number : 6909B
Ondo InsurTech PLC
23 April 2026
23 April 2026
Ondo InsurTech Plc
("Ondo" or the "Company")
Change of Board Responsibilities
Ondo InsurTech Plc (LSE: ONDO), a leading provider of claims prevention technology for home insurers, announces that Gregory Mark Wood CBE, Non-Executive Chairman of the Company, has assumed the role of Executive Chairman with immediate effect. The Board believes that Mark's extensive prior experience, including as Chief Executive Officer of Prudential UK and CEO of AXA UK, will be of considerable value to the Company in an executive function.
Mark has acted as Non-Executive Chairman since the Company's admission in 2022. This change in structure will provide additional support to Craig Foster, the Company's Chief Executive Officer, as the Company continues to pursue its commercial expansion.
Mark Wood currently owns 2,534,652 Ondo shares, representing 1.69% of the Company's issued share capital.
- Ends -
ENQUIRIES
For further information, please visit
www.ondoplc.com
or contact the following:

company_30s.what_they_do: Ondo delivers leak‑detection and repair solutions through a network of smart LeakBot devices. Homeowners and insurers use the service to spot and fix water leaks quickly, reducing damage and insurance costs.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The home‑insurance market is huge, and leak problems drive a large portion of claims. Ondo’s technology gives insurers a low‑cost way to monitor properties, creating a recurring revenue stream and a competitive edge over traditional repair services.
company_30s.current_state: Ondo has recently restructured a vendor loan note and secured a new U.S. contract, indicating a push to stabilize cash flow. Board changes and a share‑incentive plan suggest a focus on attracting talent and aligning interests, but the company is still burning cash and has not yet shown sustained profitability. The share price sits at the very bottom of its recent range.

rns_narrative: Governance overhaul: new non‑executive directors and a reshuffled board aim to bring fresh oversight and strategic direction.
Financial restructuring: the vendor loan note has been re‑issued and a share incentive plan was launched to keep key people invested.
Growth pushes: a new U.S. contract win and extensions with insurance partners signal an attempt to broaden the customer base and secure recurring revenue.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Cautiously optimistic – the chatter is low but a few voices are cheering contract wins.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Bullish posts focus on the U.S. contract and board appointments, arguing that these moves could unlock a rebound.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bearish commentary highlights the company’s cash burn, the lack of a clear path to profitability, and the risk that the restructuring may not deliver immediate value.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Over the past week sentiment has stayed flat but a few recent bullish posts suggest a small uptick in confidence after the contract announcement.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends are dead, meaning the market is largely unaware and a catalyst could trigger a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the vendor loan note restructuring – a clean balance sheet could reduce financing costs.
Announcement of additional insurance contracts, especially in the U.S., which would raise recurring revenue.
Any sign that the share incentive plan has attracted key executives and that the company is moving toward profitability.
A spike in trading volume that exceeds the 50‑day average, indicating renewed investor interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a clear break above the most recent support level, a 3‑day volume spike, or a formal funding announcement. Any of these would confirm that the market is starting to see value.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early stage of a deep crash. The APEX score suggests a tight compression, so a bounce could be triggered once capitulation is fully complete and a catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The share has fallen hard and there is no current sentiment or trend to keep it down. A bounce to halfway between the low and the old high would double the price, and a full recovery could bring a 200‑plus percent gain. The mean‑reversion thesis is strong because the business has a clear path to recurring revenue once the contracts materialise.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If the company fails to secure further funding or if the restructuring does not improve cash flow, the price could keep falling toward zero. A continued loss of confidence from insurers could also keep the stock trapped at the bottom.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 150‑200% if the price reaches the old high. Potential downside: 80‑90% if the company cannot reverse its cash burn. The asymmetry favours a bullish stance with tight risk controls.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter once the price closes above the latest support level and volume is at least 1.5× the 20‑day average. Use a position size that limits exposure to 5% of capital, and place a stop 10% below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until you see a volume spike or a clear announcement of new funding or a major contract. If either occurs, consider a small entry with a tight stop as described above.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – there are no immediate delisting or bankruptcy risks.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Ondo’s deep crash and lack of market attention create a high‑asymmetry play: a catalyst such as new contracts or a successful restructuring could trigger a strong bounce, making this a contrarian bullish setup.
